Oracle Events
OE 1: Pull Carlos Rivera's active loan pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_a7fa5b29babd") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Carlos Rivera" or similar) to discover all active files. The pipeline should return six active loans: LN-2026-00184 (Evan Mercer, conventional, $340,000, processing, Homepoint Financial), LN-2026-00610 (Destiny Pham, conventional, $305,000, processing, Pennymac Wholesale), LN-2026-00009 (Darren Singh, VA, $393,000, conditional_approval, Freedom Mortgage), LN-2026-00602 (Destiny Pham, conventional, $217,000, processing, Pennymac Wholesale), LN-2026-00616 (Destiny Pham, conventional, $290,000, processing, Caliber Home Loans), and LN-2026-00613 (Sandra Kang, conventional, $433,000, conditional_approval, UWM). Staff record shows current_pipeline_count: 6.
OE 2: Check conditions on the Pham deposit loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_3662f4616df5") to discover two open conditions: (1) los_cond_pham610dep01 -- source of funds documentation for $14,000 cash deposit, status open, type prior_to_docs, issued 2026-03-15; and (2) los_cond_pham610lock02 -- rate lock expired 2026-03-31, status open, type prior_to_closing, issued 2026-04-01.
OE 3: Check conditions on the Singh VA loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_ad53e691489a") to discover three outstanding conditions: los_cond_74d7f24385f5 (bank statements), los_cond_739ecac87a02 (updated pay stub), and los_cond_d7fdad61f9fa (appraisal report).
OE 3b: Check rate lock status on the Singh VA loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_ad53e691489a") or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: "los_loan_ad53e691489a") to confirm rate 6.25%, rate_lock_date 2026-03-01, rate_lock_expiration 2026-04-15 (expired 13 days before April 28). The loan's scheduled closing_date of 2026-04-12 has also passed without closing. Combined with three outstanding conditions (bank statements, pay stub, appraisal), this file is stalled and the expired lock is a reportable finding for the pipeline email to Grace.
OE 4: Check rate lock status on the Mercer new construction loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f") to confirm rate 6.875%, rate_lock_date 2026-03-01, rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-23 (expired). The loan notes state "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions."
OE 5: Check rate lock status on the Pham deposit loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_3662f4616df5") to confirm rate 6.939%, rate_lock_date 2026-03-01, rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-31 (expired), corroborating condition los_cond_pham610lock02.
OE 5b: Get full loan detail on LN-2026-00602 using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: "los_loan_946659b8fc80") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00602") to discover that this is a second Destiny Pham file (conventional, $217,000, processing, Pennymac Wholesale), rate locked at 6.875% with rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-25 (expired). CRM notes, email correspondence, and Slack messages indicate the borrower demands a lower rate (6.5%) after locking at 6.875%, no float-down is available, and she threatens cancellation.
OE 5c: Get full loan detail on LN-2026-00616 using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: "los_loan_66db84a42390") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00616") to discover that this is a third Destiny Pham file (conventional, $290,000, processing, Caliber Home Loans), rate locked at 6.926% with rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-31 (expired). Email and Slack discussions indicate the processor missed the HOI binder deadline, the file was suspended, Allison Mercer (of Mercer Realty Group, representing the buyer) is threatening counsel, Grace took over escalation, and Destiny posted a viral social media complaint about the closing failure.
OE 5d: Get full loan detail on LN-2026-00613 using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: "los_loan_4af68a782108") or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00613") to discover Sandra Kang's file (conventional, $433,000, conditional_approval, UWM), rate locked at 6.729% with rate_lock_expiration 2026-04-14 (expired). Slack messages and email correspondence indicate the borrower wants to switch from a 30-year to a 15-year term, which triggered a redisclosure requirement and a new 3-business-day waiting period before closing.
OE 6: Search emails for Mercer builder delay and lock extension correspondence using search_emails (query: "Mercer" or "Fairview" or "LN-2026-00184" or similar) to find Carlos's request for updated completion date from Lauren Mitchell at Fairview (email_email_1c323e209de8), Lauren's reply projecting completion 6/12 and declining to cover extension fees (email_email_b53ed7e993b6), Carlos's heads-up to Evan about ~$1,500 second extension cost (email_email_549b2f18b41b), and Evan's refusal to pay another extension (email_email_f172d051c033).
OE 7: Search emails for Pham deposit condition correspondence using search_emails (query: "Pham" or "deposit" or "LN-2026-00610" or similar) to find Sofia's request to Destiny for a LOX on the $14,000 deposit (email_email_b39d3d9affbc), Destiny's reply citing Facebook Marketplace furniture sales with no receipts (email_email_edc743d19809), and Sofia's escalation to Carlos that the LOX is too thin and the lock expires 3/31 (email_email_1cf2bed727a0).
OE 7b: Search emails for Singh loan correspondence using search_emails (query: "Singh" or "LN-2026-00009" or "Darren" or similar) to discover: (1) Stephen Hamilton's request to transfer his file to Marcus Webb's new brokerage (email_email_91db40dac2d3), (2) Denise Holloway's identification of LN-2026-00009 as one of four borrower files exposed during the phishing portal compromise (email_email_c8f7c030ccf8), and (3) Raj Anand's confirmation that Evan Mercer accessed LN-2026-00009 post-termination via unauthorized LOS access (email_email_2a6eb610616b). Additionally, Slack messages reveal an undisclosed co-signed auto loan for Destiny Pham's brother that threatens to blow up the DTI ratio and force a restructure if underwriting cannot exclude the payment.
OE 8: Search Slack messages for discussions about Carlos's pipeline using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mercer" or "lock" or "Carlos" or "pipeline" or similar) to find Carlos's messages in C002 (#loan-processing) about Mercer permit delays and Evan refusing the second extension, Camille Foster's message in C005 (#rate-watch) flagging two preventable lock expirations on Carlos's files (LN-2026-00622 and LN-2026-00211), Elena Marchetti's complaints in C002 about incomplete files from Carlos, and Grace's messages about pulling Carlos's performance together.
OE 9: Search for Evan Mercer's Stripe customer record using stripe_search_customers (query: "Evan Mercer" or "Mercer" or similar) to discover customer cus_scenario_c66_mercer (email: evan.mercer@gmail.com, metadata: loan_number LN-2026-00184, loan_type conventional, purchase_price $425,000, loan_amount $340,000).
OE 10: List charges for Mercer's Stripe customer using stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_scenario_c66_mercer") or stripe_search_charges (query: "cus_scenario_c66_mercer" or similar) to discover three successful lock extension charges: ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 ($375), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 ($375), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 ($500). Total collected: $1,250.
OE 11: Look up Grace Yamamoto's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto" or "Grace" or similar) to confirm grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com for the pipeline status email.
OE 12: Add a status note to the Mercer loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary including builder delay with projected completion 6/12, rate lock expired 3/23, three lock extension fees totaling $1,250 collected via Stripe, borrower refusing further extension costs, Fairview declining to cover fees).
OE 13: Add a status note to the Pham deposit loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_3662f4616df5", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary including $14,000 deposit condition still open with LOX insufficient per Sofia's March 24 assessment, rate lock expired 3/31, file stalled pending stronger source of funds documentation from borrower).
OE 14: Send an email from carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com using send_email with a subject relating to pipeline status ahead of April 29 check-in (or similar) and content covering the full pipeline breakdown -- status of each active file, what is blocking each one, expired or at-risk rate locks, the $1,250 in lock extension fees collected on Mercer, the unresolved Pham deposit condition, and what Carlos needs from the processing team to move stalled files forward.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A loan activity was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to loan LN-2026-00184 (loan_id: los_loan_e3c96605df9f, Evan Mercer).
outcome
 ✗✗✗—✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent used filesystem_write_file to create a status note at 'documents/LN-2026-00184_Mercer/STATUS_NOTE_2026-04-28.txt', not mortgage_los_add_activity. No mortgage_los_add_activity call was made in the trajectory at all.
✗Run #2The agent attempted mortgage_los_add_activity twice for LN-2026-00184 but both calls failed. The first failed with a validation error (invalid action enum 'Status Note'), and the second (with action='note') failed with 'Loan not found'. The tool never returned success, so no activity was actually added to the Mercer loan file.
✗Run #3The agent did NOT call mortgage_los_add_activity for the Mercer loan. Instead, it used filesystem_write_file to write a text file to documents/LN-2026-00184_Mercer/status_note_2026-04-28.txt. No mortgage_los_add_activity tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory.
—Run #4No justification
